questions.text: Is Al-Fatihah the first Surah revealed in order of revelation?
Why is it referred to as 'Umm al-Kitab' (Mother of the Book)?
In 'Alhamdulillah', who does the praise truly belong to and why?
Explain the linguistic depth of 'Ar-Rahman' vs 'Ar-Rahim'.
Does 'Maliki Yawm id-Din' imply God only rules on Judgment Day?
How does 'Iyyaka na'budu' shift the perspective from the third person to the second person?
What is the grammatical significance of the 'n' in 'na'budu'?
Is 'Ihdinas-siratal-mustaqim' a request for guidance or a request for steadfastness?
Who are 'those who have evoked Your anger' historically and conceptually?
Why is 'Amin' not written in the Mushaf if it is part of the prayer?
How does Al-Fatihah summarize the entire message of the Quran?
Can a prayer be valid without the recitation of Al-Fatihah?
What is the relationship between 'Rububiyyah' and the first verse?
How does this Surah bridge the gap between the Creator and the creation?
What are the practical implications of acknowledging Allah as 'Malik' in daily life?
